design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate platforming stages as Batman, utilizing gadgets and melee combat to defeat themed enemies and bosses representing iconic villains from Batman: The Animated Series
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (limited equipment ammunition) versus combat engagement; gadget procurement gates progression through themed obstacles
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Embody Batman from the animated series, employing his signature gadgets (batarangs, grappling hook, smoke bombs, x-ray glasses) across visually distinct Gotham locations inspired by art deco aesthetic
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strict commitment to animated series visual fidelity and tone; gadget-gated level design where stage completion requires specific equipment rather than pure platforming skill; high reliance on resource conservation

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives system with respawn at death location; multiple extra lives distributed throughout stages
difficulty.continues_system: Infinite continues available; score resets to zero upon continue (penalty encourages full playthroughs without continues)
difficulty.checkpointing: Instant respawn at death point with full health bar and ammunition intact; defeated enemies remain defeated within checkpoint segment
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Three selectable difficulty modes (Easy, Normal, Hard) accessible via hidden level select; hidden stage select allows non-linear progression and difficulty testing

progression.upgrade_system: Equipment-based: Batman acquires limited-use gadgets throughout stages (throwing stars, smoke bombs, flashlights, x-ray glasses, gas masks); gadgets serve both combat and environmental puzzle functions
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage progression unlocks via sequential level completion; gadgets unlocked through item discovery within stages; hidden level select bypasses linear progression
replayability_hooks: Three difficulty modes alter enemy placement and health
Hidden level select enables stage selection and skill testing
Score attack motivation (continue penalty encourages no-death runs)

world_building_techniques: Themed stages representing villain domains (each stage dedicated to specific Batman: The Animated Series antagonist)
Art deco visual design maintaining animated series aesthetic continuity
Enemy and boss selection drawn from show's rogues gallery (Joker, Two-Face, villain-themed stage environments)

boss_design.philosophy: Iconic villain encounters serving as stage climax and thematic payoff; bosses represent named Batman: The Animated Series antagonists rather than generic threats
notable_bosses: Stage-specific villain representatives implied by themed environments and level structure

faction_indicators: Batman as protagonist represents law enforcement/justice archetype
Recurring villain roster (Joker, Two-Face, etc.) establishes antagonistic faction
danger_signals: Escalating enemy complexity across stage progression
Boss encounters framed as criminal mastermind confrontations
Gadget depletion as resource scarcity threat
environmental_storytelling: Themed stage environments (theater, office building, graveyard, amusement park in Game Gear version) establish villain territory and motivation
Art deco visual language communicates Gotham's noir aesthetic consistent with animated series
Gadget necessity for stage progression (flashlights, x-ray glasses, gas masks) telegraphs environmental hazards and stage design intent

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Batman & Robin SNES page)
wikipedia (The Adventures of Batman & Robin video game page)
